pages.raw_text:
WASHINGTON, Feb. 17.-Dear
Dad: 1t is time for the little fell
lows, the plain citizens, to look into
this business of state rights.


Water power is the bullseye of
the Ballinger-Pinchot inquiry tar
get. There is battle on over the
coal in Alaska, and the public for
ests and the public domain. Long
after that Alaskan coal has been
burned, however, the country will
be depending on the water power
that constitutes the real and rich
est prize in this fight between a
few rich grabbers and the people.


The water power grabbers, who
are hiding behind the row that is
raised over Alaska coal, have
seized about all the power sites ex-
cept such as are on public lands.


When the question comes up
Senator Nelson and lot more, to-
gether with a rich lobby and fleet
of newspapers all over the country,
will argue that all water not navi-
gable is the property of the state
within which the water runs. The
General Electric expects to get this
water power if it is turned over to
the states.
